content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers N and K, where N is the size of the box and K is the maximum number of cells that can be different. The next N blocks contain N lines, each line containing N numbers, representing the values of the cells in the box.
content.outputFormat: The output should be two numbers, the minimum and maximum possible values of F(D) among all boxes D that have the same dimensions as A and differ in no more than K cells.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The box is homogeneous, so the compression level is 1.
We can change one cell to get a box with compression level 8.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the compression level of the box for all possible boxes that differ in no more than K cells. We can use a recursive approach to calculate the compression level of each box. The base case is when the box is homogeneous, in which case the compression level is 1. Otherwise, we divide the box into 8 smaller boxes and calculate the compression level of each smaller box recursively.
solution.timeComplexity: O(2^(N^3)) — the time complexity is exponential in the size of the box, because in the worst case, we need to calculate the compression level of all possible boxes.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^3) — the space complexity is cubic in the size of the box, because we need to store the values of all cells in the box.
